subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear title and introduction that orient the reader immediately
Logical flow through introduction, data presentation, analysis, and conclusion
Effective figures (e.g., experimental setup) that support understanding of results
weaknesses: Frequent formatting errors (duplicated headings, garbled tables) disrupt coherence
Redundant discussions of the same formula and data interrupt conciseness
Conclusion is truncated and lacks a clear, full summary of findings
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows some coherence and logical development with a recognisable flow (band 2) and an adequate overall structure (band 2). While most content relates to the research question, unnecessary repetition and formatting issues prevent it from being concise and highly organized. These observations align squarely with band 2 (âsome coherence in its developmentâ, âadequate organization with basic structureâ, âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ).

strengths: Use of multiple representations (raw data tables, scatter plots, schematic diagrams) is effective and aids comprehension
Correct use of LaTeX for key calculations shows strong mathematical language capability
Figures such as the mean-volume scatterplot are well annotated and reinforce the analysis
weaknesses: Notation is sometimes imprecise (inconsistent units, tcdf syntax lacks parameters)
Logical gaps occur in the interpretation of statistical tests (assumptions not checked before inference)
Table headers occasionally lack unit labels, reducing clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates effective use of multiple forms of representation (band 3) and mostly consistent use of appropriate mathematical language (borderline band 3), with good logical structure and clear communication in many sections (band 3). Occasional notation lapses and clarity issues prevent a band 4 rating, but overall the communication is solidly in band 3. Thus a score of 3/4 reflects this performance.

strengths: Choice of a baking experiment shows clear personal interest and genuine engagement
Extension of the standard experiment (adding volume measurements) demonstrates significant independent thinking
Original design of the data-collection procedure indicates good creativity and initiative
weaknesses: Approach, while personal, follows largely standard investigative steps without exceptional innovation
Analysis methods remain textbook-based, limiting the depth of personal contribution
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evidence of personal engagement is clear through an original baking context and added volume analysis, showing significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach (band 2). Creativity and initiative are present but not at an outstanding level, matching band 2 criteria for this section.

strengths: Acknowledgement of measurement errors and basic limitations shows some reflective intent
Short discussion of statistical significance indicates initial evaluation efforts
weaknesses: Reflection is shallow and sporadic, with minimal probing of underlying causes or alternative approaches
Evaluation of outcomes is limited to surface conclusions and lacks suggestions for future work
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reflection is present but remains at a basic level, offering limited critique of the process and outcomes (band 1). Analysis stays at a surface level with minimal evaluation of implications or future directions, consistent with a 1/3 score in this criterion.

strengths: Relevant mathematics (averages, correlation, t-test, volume calculations) is applied appropriately to the research question
Methods align well with SL course expectations, demonstrating solid level appropriateness
Most calculations are correct and support the analysis effectively
weaknesses: Some rounding inaccuracies and misâsubstitution in the t-test degrees of freedom introduce minor errors
Volume formula is applied without derivation or clear definition of variables
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student uses relevant mathematics appropriately (band 3) and employs techniques that are commensurate with SL level (band 3). Understanding is demonstrated with mostly correct work, though minor errors prevent a higher band. A score of 3/6 fairly reflects the competent but not expert use of mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations of the analytical steps are readable but contain logical gaps (e.g. the leap from r to approving the hypothesis without checking model assumptions). Thus clarity is intermittent, fitting band 2 (âsome logical structure and clarityâ).

Calculations of r and t appear correct, yet the volume formula is applied without derivation and some rounding/formatting inaccuracies occur. The student shows understanding but with errors, which aligns with band 2.

Raw-data tables, averaged-value tables, three scatter plots and a schematic diagram give several representations that help understanding even though some axes lack scale and one table is garbled. This meets âeffective useâ (band 3) rather than highly effective.

The investigation follows a recognisable flow (introduction â data â analysis â conclusion) so the reader can follow the argument, yet sections are uneven and frequently interrupted by formatting errors, duplicated headings and fragmented sentences. Ideas sometimes jumpâfor example, averageâheight calculations appear before raw data are fully presentedâso the line of reasoning is only partially smooth. This aligns best with âsome coherence in its development.â
Introducing volume as an extra measure and attempting a t-test indicate initiative, though methods remain standard; creativity is good but not exceptional, corresponding to band 2.

The student comments on statistical significance and acknowledges measurement errors, but the analysis stays surface-level without probing underlying causes or alternative modelsâconsistent with âbasic analysisâ (band 1).
Outcome evaluation is confined to confirming the hypothesis and noting percentage differences; recommendations are minimal. This is âlimited evaluation,â thus band 1.
Reflection is present mainly in the limitations paragraph and brief comparison of mean points; depth is shallow and sporadic, indicating âlimited reflectionâ (band 1).

The student uses correct symbols for fractions, r-value, t-test formula and volume, yet notation is sometimes imprecise (e.g. tcdf syntax, inconsistent use of units, unclear vector bolding). Overall this shows âsomeâ but not consistent appropriate language.
The study is clearly shaped by the studentâs personal interest in baking; choice of recipe and variables reflects a personal approach rather than a generic canned investigation, satisfying band 2.

Most material relates to the research question, but lengthy recipe anecdotes, repeated height averages, and verbose explanations of well-known statistical ideas reduce conciseness. Hence the work is âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elements,â placing it in band 2.

All mathematics (averages, scatter plots, Pearson correlation, t-test, triangular-prism volume) is relevant to the question, though at a modest level. This matches the description ârelevant mathematics used appropriatelyâ (band 3).

Designing and carrying out an original baking experiment and choosing to test a statistical relationship shows noticeable independent thinking beyond following textbook examples, meriting âsignificant evidenceâ (band 2).

There is a basic structure with clear headings, but tables break across pages, figures lack consistent numbering and captions, and the data/analysis discussion is scattered. An introduction and conclusion exist, yet a formal body sectioning is loose. Such âadequate organisation with basic structureâ corresponds to band 2.
The methods (correlation, t-test, 3-D volume) sit comfortably within SL expectations; no higher-level techniques are attempted, so âcommensurate with course levelâ (band 3) is appropriate.

annotations.comment: The title clearly states the investigationâs focus, providing immediate context and direction for the reader.
Consider sharpening the research question by phrasing it explicitly (e.g., âTo what extent does X tsp of baking soda affect scone height?â) to improve focus.
The introduction conveys purpose and context, but the central question could be restated more succinctly to guide the structure of the investigation.
Linking your personal hobby to the topic strengthens engagement, showing clear evidence of genuine interest.
The citation for the recipe lacks consistent formattingâuse a standard style (APA or MLA) and include access dates.
The volume formula notation is introduced without derivation or clear definition of variables a, b, c. Provide context or a derivation for full understanding.
Raw data is described before it is presented; consider presenting the table first, then interpreting it to improve logical flow.
Table headers lack unit labels; include âcmâ beside each column heading for clarity and consistency in notation.
The large, complex table on page 4 is garbled and distracts from understanding; simplify or split into clearer components.
Figure 1 is useful but lacks consistent numbering/placement. Ensure captions and figure numbers appear uniformly.
Averageâheight calculations are clearly shown using LaTeX, demonstrating accurate and consistent mathematical language.
Rounded averages are said to two significant figures, yet some values appear overârounded; verify each figureâs justification.
The scatterplots in Figure 2 effectively illustrate trends in height vs. soda amount, supporting visual analysis.
The methodology describes use of a Bonferroni correction without justification; explain why itâs appropriate for this dataset.
Degrees of freedom in t-test formula are mis-substituted; with n=18 df should be 16, not 20. Recheck the calculation.
The tcdf notation is inconsistent and lacks degrees-of-freedom input; present p-values in conventional terms or show full parameter list.
The volume diagram adds clarity, but its caption is unnumbered; align numbering with previous figures for consistency.
The volume formula is repeated; consolidate discussions to avoid redundancy and improve conciseness.
Multiple raw data tables spanning three pages disrupt flow; consider combining or summarizing to maintain coherence.
Figure 6 presents a clear scatter of mean volumes, effectively reinforcing observed trends.
The conclusion is truncated mid-sentence (âno significantâ); ensure full statements and clear summary of findings.
Findings are summarized but lack discussion of potential future work or broader implications; consider adding this for depth.
The limitations section reflects on measurement and timing errors thoughtfully, demonstrating critical analysis of the process.
Figure 7 effectively illustrates the experimental setup and outcomes; the image supports understanding of results.
Bibliography entries vary in format and lack consistent punctuation and italics; apply a uniform reference style throughout.
